name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
أَوَكُلَّمَا عَـٰهَدُوا۟ عَهْدًا نَّبَذَهُۥ فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُم ۚ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (١٠٠)
```

```english
Is it not that whenever they made a covenant, a part of them would cast it away? Rather, the majority of them do not have faith. (100)
```

```arabic
وَلَمَّا جَآءَهُمْ رَسُولٌ مِّنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ مُصَدِّقٌ لِّمَا مَعَهُمْ نَبَذَ فَرِيقٌ مِّنَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ كِتَـٰبَ ٱللَّهِ وَرَآءَ ظُهُورِهِمْ كَأَنَّهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (١٠١)
```

```english
And when there came to them an apostle from Allah, confirming that which is with them, a part of those who were given the Book cast the Book of Allah behind their back, as if they did not know [that it is Allah’s Book]. (101)
```

## (Commentary)

**His saying, the Exalted:** "flung it aside" (نَبَذَهُ). To fling aside (النبذ) means to cast away or discard.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَلَمَّا جَاءَهُمْ رَسُولٌ** "And when there came to them an apostle" — the intended meaning here is the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله‌وسلم), not every messenger who came to them confirming what was with them. This is because the phrasing "And when there came to them" (ولما جائهم) does not denote continuity or repetition, but rather indicates a single, specific occurrence. The verse refers to their opposition to the truth (الحق) manifested in their concealment of the glad tidings found in the Torah (التوراة) and their refusal to believe in the one who confirms what is already with them.
